milestones.title: Auto Rule Generation from Feedback
milestones.vision: Turn positive review feedback into durable active rules that shape future reviews through a bounded, sanitized, observable lifecycle.
success_criteria: Generated rules persist separately from learning memories with bounded lifecycle state.
A clustering sweep produces pending proposals and auto-activates high-confidence rules.
Active rules are sanitized and injected into future review prompts through a bounded path.
Retirement and notification behavior are fail-open and machine-checkable.

key_risks.risk: Sparse or noisy clusters generate low-quality rules.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Bad generated rules would poison the prompt rather than improve future reviews.

key_risks.risk: Generated rule text becomes a prompt-injection surface.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Auto-generated instructions must be sanitized and tightly bounded before they reach the model.

key_risks.risk: Lifecycle logic becomes sticky and keeps stale rules active too long.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Rules should reflect current team preferences, not frozen historical artifacts.

key_risks.risk: M036 scope bleeds into M037's per-review scoring path.
key_risks.whyItMatters: M036 should produce durable rules; M037 handles ephemeral reinforcement at review time.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can Kodiai persist generated-rule lifecycle state and derive proposal candidates from clustered learning memories?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Schema/store plus clustering tests prove positive-signal clusters can become bounded pending-rule proposals.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Will active generated rules actually shape future reviews through the prompt path?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Activation and prompt-injection proof shows sanitized active rules appear in the review prompt and remain bounded.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can rules retire cleanly and notify operators without blocking review execution?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Retirement, notification, and verifier paths prove stale or degraded rules can be retired fail-open with machine-checkable evidence.

milestones.verification_contract: Unit and fixture verification must prove proposal generation, activation, prompt injection, and retirement all operate through bounded, sanitized, fail-open paths.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration proof must show a repo with sufficient learning-memory history generating at least one active rule that appears in the next review prompt.
milestones.verification_operational: Operational verification must demonstrate activation/retirement observability, bounded rule injection, and non-blocking behavior when generation or lookup fails.
milestones.verification_uat: A user should be able to observe a recurring accepted pattern become an active rule and then see that rule appear in future review prompts; if the pattern degrades, the rule should retire without breaking reviews.
definition_of_done: Generated rules persist separately from raw learning memories with explicit pending/active/retired lifecycle state.
A sweep can cluster positive feedback history into rule proposals and auto-activate high-confidence rules.
Active rules are injected into the review prompt through a bounded, sanitized path.
Retirement and notification behavior are machine-checkable and fail-open.
milestones.requirement_coverage: This milestone establishes the durable feedback-to-rule loop that M037 depends on. It clusters positive feedback into rule proposals, activates high-confidence rules, injects active rules into the prompt, and retires stale rules.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **Owns:** generated rule storage, clustering/proposal sweep, activation/retirement lifecycle, and prompt injection of active rules
- **Consumes:** learning memories, existing clustering helpers, review prompt custom-instructions surface, and optional Slack notification path
- **Provides to downstream milestones:** durable active rules derived from feedback history for M037 and future review behavior
- **Does not own:** per-review ephemeral scoring (M037) or graph/current-code substrate work

milestones.title: Embedding-Based Suggestion Clustering & Reinforcement Learning
milestones.vision: Use cached positive and negative feedback clusters to make review-time finding suppression and confidence adjustments thematic, conservative, and non-blocking.
success_criteria: Per-repo positive/negative cluster models persist with freshness metadata and bounded refresh.
Review-time findings can be thematically suppressed or boosted based on cached cluster models.
Safety guards keep severe findings from being wrongly suppressed and preserve conservative behavior.
Stale or missing models fail open and do not block review completion.

key_risks.risk: Cluster similarity suppresses real findings that only look thematically similar to past noise.
key_risks.whyItMatters: False suppression would silently reduce review quality, so thresholds and safety guards must be conservative.

key_risks.risk: Per-review scoring becomes expensive if the cluster model is rebuilt too often.
key_risks.whyItMatters: M037 should use cached models, not turn every review into a clustering job.

key_risks.risk: Positive reinforcement boosts low-quality patterns the team happened to accept historically.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Confidence increases need guardrails, especially around severe findings.

key_risks.risk: M037 duplicates M036's durable rule behavior instead of staying ephemeral.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The milestone should score findings in-flight, not mutate the prompt contract or rule lifecycle.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can Kodiai build and cache useful positive/negative cluster models from learning memories without doing the work on every review?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Model-build and cache tests prove per-repo cluster models can be persisted and refreshed in a bounded background path.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Will cluster models actually change review-time confidence and suppression behavior in a controlled way?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Scoring and review integration proof shows thematic negative clusters suppress eligible findings and positive clusters boost confidence without bypassing safety guards.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can the refresh and fail-open paths keep the system operational when cluster data is stale or unavailable?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Refresh and verifier paths prove cached reuse, stale-model handling, and non-blocking review behavior under failure.

milestones.verification_contract: Unit and fixture verification must prove cluster models are cached, scoring is conservative and safety-guarded, and refresh/degradation paths stay non-blocking.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration proof must show a review run whose findings change meaningfully under cached positive/negative cluster models compared with the naive path.
milestones.verification_operational: Operational verification must demonstrate bounded background refresh, stale-model handling, cached reuse, and explicit fail-open behavior.
milestones.verification_uat: A user should be able to review a PR in a repo with enough history and see that repeated low-value thematic findings are suppressed while high-value thematic findings gain confidence, without CRITICAL findings disappearing and without review failures when models are unavailable.
definition_of_done: Per-repo cluster models persist separately from durable generated rules.
Review-time finding scoring can suppress or boost based on thematic positive/negative clusters.
Critical-safety and fail-open behavior are explicit and tested.
Background refresh and verifier paths keep model use bounded and observable.
milestones.requirement_coverage: This milestone consumes the durable feedback foundation from M036 and adds ephemeral per-review reinforcement: cached cluster models, thematic suppression/boost scoring, safety-guarded integration, and bounded refresh/verification.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **Owns:** per-repo positive/negative cluster model build/cache, per-review finding scoring, suppression/boost adjustment logic, and refresh/verification for ephemeral reinforcement behavior
- **Consumes:** learning memories, existing cluster helpers, review findings pipeline, safety-guard logic, and durable active rules from M036
- **Provides to downstream review flow:** thematic suppression/boost signals at review time
- **Does not own:** durable rule lifecycle (M036) or structural/current-code substrate work

milestones.title: AST Call-Graph Impact Analysis
milestones.vision: Consume M040's graph substrate and M041's canonical current-code corpus at review time so Kodiai can show bounded, structurally-grounded impact evidence and more truthful breaking-change output for C++ and Python PRs.
success_criteria: M038 consumes M040 and M041 through explicit review-time adapters rather than duplicating substrate logic.
Review Details shows a bounded Structural Impact section for C++ and Python scenarios using graph and canonical current-code evidence.
Breaking-change output cites structural caller/dependent evidence when available and degrades truthfully when unavailable.
Repeated requests reuse cached structural-impact results and timeout/failure paths fail open.

key_risks.risk: M040 and M041 query surfaces may drift, making M038 tightly coupled to unstable substrate APIs.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the consumer contracts are unclear, M038 will either stall or re-implement substrate logic locally.

key_risks.risk: Structural impact formatting may over-pack graph and corpus context, hurting prompt quality.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The consumer only helps if it produces bounded, ranked evidence rather than a noisy blast-radius dump.

key_risks.risk: Breaking-change evidence may overstate certainty for C++/Python when graph resolution is probabilistic.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Review output must stay truthful about structural confidence, especially for C++ macros/templates and Python dynamic dispatch.

key_risks.risk: Timeout or substrate failures could accidentally block review completion.
key_risks.whyItMatters: M038 sits directly in the review path, so fail-open behavior is mandatory.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can M038 consume M040/M041 through explicit adapter surfaces rather than reaching into substrate internals?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Query adapters and orchestration tests prove review-time requests can fetch blast radius and unchanged-code evidence through stable, bounded consumer contracts.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Will the consumed graph/corpus evidence actually improve user-visible Review Details and breaking-change output?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Structural Impact formatting and handler integration prove C++/Python review flows surface caller/dependent evidence and bounded unchanged-code context in Review Details.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can the review-time consumer remain truthful, bounded, cached, and fail-open under timeout and degradation paths?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: End-to-end verifier covers cached reuse, timeout/failure degradation, bounded output, and evidence-backed breaking-change behavior without blocking the review.

milestones.verification_contract: Unit and fixture verification must prove M038 consumes substrate APIs, bounds its structural-impact payload, and never blocks review completion when graph or corpus data is unavailable.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration proof must show graph blast radius plus canonical unchanged-code evidence flowing into Review Details and breaking-change output for C++ or Python review fixtures.
milestones.verification_operational: Operational verification must demonstrate timeout behavior, cache reuse for repeated commit pairs, explicit degradation signals, and fail-open review completion.
milestones.verification_uat: A user should be able to review a large C++ or Python PR and see a bounded Structural Impact section with caller/dependent evidence, then rerun the same review and observe cached reuse; if substrate data is unavailable, the review still completes without invented structural claims.
definition_of_done: M038 consumes stable query surfaces from M040 and M041 rather than rebuilding substrate logic locally.
Review Details can show a bounded Structural Impact section for C++ and Python PRs with caller/dependent/blast-radius evidence.
Breaking-change output uses structural evidence when available and degrades truthfully when unavailable.
Timeout, cache, and fail-open behavior are machine-checkable and non-blocking.
milestones.requirement_coverage: R037 is advanced by S01 and S02 through graph/corpus consumption and Structural Impact rendering. R038 is advanced by S02 and S03 through evidence-backed breaking-change output plus timeout/fail-open behavior.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **Owns:** review-time orchestration that queries M040 graph APIs and M041 canonical corpus APIs, bounded structural-impact formatting, and breaking-change evidence surfaces in the review flow
- **Consumes:** M040 graph substrate, M041 canonical current-code corpus, `src/handlers/review.ts`, and `src/execution/review-prompt.ts`
- **Provides:** structurally-grounded Review Details context and evidence-backed breaking-change signals to end users
- **Does not own:** graph extraction/storage from M040 or canonical code-corpus storage/backfill/update from M041

milestones.title: Review Output Hardening — Intent Parsing + Claude Usage Visibility
milestones.vision: Harden two already-shipped review output surfaces so they reflect real signals truthfully: fix PR body template stripping so boilerplate cannot trigger false breaking-change intent, and change the Claude usage display to show percent-left with a truthful fallback when the SDK does not emit rate-limit data.
success_criteria: A checked-in xbmc-style PR body fixture does NOT trigger `breaking change in body` after stripping — the existing heading-only removal is extended to strip section body content through the next heading
Plain author-prose `breaking change` in body is still detected after the improved stripping
Review Details Claude usage line renders percent-left and reset timing when execution results carry rate-limit data
When execution results lack rate-limit data, Review Details omits the usage line or renders a truthful unavailable state — no invented numbers
Deterministic regression tests in `src/lib/pr-intent-parser.test.ts`, `src/lib/review-utils.test.ts`, and `src/handlers/review.test.ts` lock both contracts down
bun run tsc --noEmit exits clean

key_risks.risk: PR template stripping is repo-specific — heading names and checkbox structure vary across repos
key_risks.whyItMatters: Overly narrow stripping only fixes the specific xbmc pattern; overly broad stripping suppresses real breaking-change signals in non-template bodies

key_risks.risk: Claude SDK rate_limit_event emission is not guaranteed for every run
key_risks.whyItMatters: The percent-left display must define a truthful fallback rather than silently showing nothing or fabricating usage data

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Template stripping breadth vs precision
proof_strategy.retireIn: S01 — xbmc fixture test and preserved plain-prose detection test
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A minimal real-world xbmc PR body is stripped correctly AND a genuine body-prose breaking-change signal still fires

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Percent-left display and fallback behavior
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Usage line renders percent-left when rate-limit data present; line is absent or explicit when data absent

milestones.verification_contract: Parser tests: `bun test ./src/lib/pr-intent-parser.test.ts` — fixture test passes, plain-prose test passes, existing tests unchanged. Review-utils tests: `bun test ./src/lib/review-utils.test.ts` — percent-left contract verified. Handler wiring tests: `bun test ./src/handlers/review.test.ts` — relevant wiring expectations updated.
milestones.verification_integration: `bun run tsc --noEmit` exits clean across all changed files.
milestones.verification_operational: When execution result carries rate-limit data: Review Details shows `Claude Code usage: XX% of seven_day limit remaining | resets ...`. When execution result lacks rate-limit data: usage line is absent from Review Details output.
milestones.verification_uat: A real PR body matching the xbmc template structure no longer shows `breaking change in body` in Review Details. An execution result with `usageLimit.utilization = 0.75` shows approximately `25% remaining` (not 75% used). An execution result without `usageLimit` shows no Claude usage line.
definition_of_done: S01 and S02 complete and verified
xbmc fixture regression test passes
percent-left display contract locked in review-utils test
bun run tsc --noEmit exits clean
No regressions in existing parser, review-utils, or handler tests
milestones.requirement_coverage: No existing requirements cover these surfaces. Both fixes harden already-shipped contracts: parser template stripping and Claude usage display. This milestone introduces the correctness requirements for both.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
```
pr-intent-parser.ts (stripTemplateBoilerplate)
  → extended: strips heading-body content through next heading
  → new: xbmc fixture test
review-utils.ts (formatReviewDetailsSummary)
  → changed: percent-left display, truthful fallback
  → test: review-utils.test.ts contract updated
handlers/review.ts
  → unchanged wiring — passes usageLimit through as before
  → test: review.test.ts expectations updated
```

milestones.title: Graph-Backed Extensive Review Context
milestones.vision: Build a persistent structural graph for C++ and Python first — with TS/JS secondary — so Kodiai can compute blast radius, likely affected tests, and bounded graph-aware review context for extensive PRs without regressing normal review cost.
success_criteria: Kodiai can persist and incrementally update a structural graph for fixture C++ and Python repos using Tree-sitter-backed extraction.
Graph queries surface impacted files, probable callers/dependents, and likely tests beyond today's file-level risk triage.
Large/extensive review selection uses bounded graph-derived ranking while trivial/small PRs bypass or bound graph overhead.
Graph-amplified context and optional validation fail open and do not block review completion.

key_risks.risk: Tree-sitter extraction over-recalls or under-resolves C++ and Python structure, producing noisy blast radius results.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If extraction quality is weak, the graph will make review context noisier instead of more useful.

key_risks.risk: Graph build/query work regresses latency or token use on small PRs.
key_risks.whyItMatters: M040 only helps if it improves extensive reviews without making normal reviews slower or more expensive.

key_risks.risk: Prompt integration dumps too much graph context and overwhelms downstream review quality.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Blast radius is valuable only if it is ranked and bounded before reaching the LLM.

key_risks.risk: Validation gate or graph failure paths become blocking in production.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Graph work must be additive and fail-open, not a new single point of review failure.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can Kodiai extract and persist useful structural graph data for C++ and Python on its existing stack?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Dedicated graph schema, extractors, and incremental graph-build tests prove fixture repos can be indexed and updated without a separate graph store.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Does graph data actually improve extensive-review selection versus current file-level triage?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Fixture-based blast-radius and review-selection proof shows impacted files/tests/dependents are found beyond current triage while context remains ranked.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can graph context stay bounded, skip trivial PRs, and fail open under production-like conditions?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: End-to-end verification proves trivial-change bypass, bounded prompt packing, optional validation behavior, and fail-open review completion.

milestones.verification_contract: Fixture and unit verification must prove graph extraction, storage, and query results are correct enough to improve review selection on C++/Python-first repos without replacing the existing retrieval stack.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration proof must show graph-derived impacted files/tests/dependents flowing into review selection and prompt assembly on a production-like large PR fixture.
milestones.verification_operational: Operational verification must demonstrate incremental graph updates, explicit bypass for trivial changes, bounded context ranking, and fail-open handling for graph or validation failures.
milestones.verification_uat: A user should be able to compare a large C++ or Python PR before and after graph integration and see a smaller, better-targeted review context set with explicit impacted files/tests, while a trivial PR still runs on the cheap path.
definition_of_done: Persistent graph tables and extraction/update paths exist on Kodiai's current stack.
C++ and Python extraction/query fixtures prove blast-radius usefulness; TS/JS remain secondary.
Review selection and prompt assembly consume bounded graph signals rather than raw graph dumps.
Trivial PR bypass and fail-open behavior are tested and machine-checkable.
Optional validation for graph-amplified findings is configurable and bounded.
milestones.requirement_coverage: R033 is advanced by S01 and S02 through persistent graph storage plus graph-aware extensive-review selection. R034 is advanced primarily by S03 through bounded context ranking, trivial-change bypass, and fail-open validation/graph behavior.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **Owns:** structural graph schema, extraction/update jobs, blast-radius queries, graph-aware review selection, bounded graph prompt section, and optional graph-amplified validation gate
- **Consumes:** repo contents from `src/jobs/workspace.ts`, existing large-PR triage surfaces in `src/lib/file-risk-scorer.ts`, prompt assembly in `src/execution/review-prompt.ts`
- **Provides to downstream milestones:** persistent graph substrate and graph-ranked impacted context for M038 to consume
- **Does not own:** canonical current-code corpus from M041, historical snippet retrieval, or AST/call-graph consumer behavior specific to M038

milestones.title: Canonical Repo-Code Corpus
milestones.vision: Build a canonical default-branch code corpus for current unchanged code — separate from historical diff hunks — with truthful provenance, selective upkeep, and semantic retrieval that downstream review systems can trust.
success_criteria: Backfill persists canonical current-code chunks for the default branch with truthful repo/ref/commit provenance.
Semantic retrieval over the canonical corpus returns current unchanged code rather than historical PR diff hunks.
Normal freshness updates are selective: unchanged files and unchanged chunks are not re-embedded.
Audit/repair detects and repairs stale, missing, or model-mismatched canonical rows without full-repo re-embedding.

key_risks.risk: Chunk identity and replacement semantics drift under refactors, causing duplicate or orphaned canonical rows.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If replacement semantics are unstable, incremental updates stop being trustworthy and the corpus loses provenance integrity.

key_risks.risk: Scope bleeds into graph or prompt-consumer work that belongs to M040 or M038.
key_risks.whyItMatters: That would blur milestone boundaries and delay the substrate without improving its core contract.

key_risks.risk: Merge/update freshness signals are incomplete or delayed, leaving the canonical corpus behind HEAD.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The corpus is only useful if it stays close to current default-branch truth.

key_risks.risk: Corpus growth and exclusion policy are too loose, leading to wasteful embeddings for generated or low-value files.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Unbounded corpus size increases cost, retrieval noise, and repair work.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Stable replacement semantics and separation from historical snippet storage
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Fixture ingest plus unit tests prove dedicated canonical tables, chunk identity, and replacement behavior without touching historical diff-hunk rows.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Backfill usefulness and truthful retrieval from current code at HEAD
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A production-like repo snapshot can be backfilled once and queried for semantically relevant unchanged code with full provenance.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Freshness and bounded upkeep without full-repo rebuilds
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Changed-file refresh plus audit/repair proves selective updates, stale-row detection, and model-mismatch repair under fail-open conditions.

milestones.verification_contract: Unit and fixture verification must prove canonical storage, chunking, and retrieval are separate from the historical diff-hunk corpus and preserve truthful provenance on every returned chunk.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration proof must show one-time backfill, review-style retrieval, changed-file refresh, and selective repair working together on a production-like repo snapshot.
milestones.verification_operational: Operational verification must demonstrate bounded update behavior, explicit stale/missing/model-mismatch reporting, and fail-open handling when embedding or file parsing fails.
milestones.verification_uat: A user or operator should be able to point at a repo, backfill the default branch once, query for unchanged code relevant to a review scenario, then observe that later updates touch only changed files while audit/repair closes any drift.
definition_of_done: Canonical current-code chunks persist separately from historical diff-hunk snippets and carry repo/ref/commit provenance.
Default-branch backfill works for a production-like repo and retrieval returns current unchanged code from the canonical corpus.
Incremental update paths touch only changed files or changed chunks in the normal case.
Audit/repair detects stale, missing, or model-mismatched rows and repairs them selectively.
Failure paths are fail-open and bounded; reviews are not blocked by corpus upkeep problems.
milestones.requirement_coverage: R036 is advanced by S01 and S02 through canonical storage, provenance, and semantic retrieval over current unchanged code. R035 is advanced by S03 through selective changed-file refresh plus bounded audit/repair instead of periodic full-repo re-embedding.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **Owns:** canonical current-code corpus tables, chunking/exclusion rules, backfill/update/audit jobs, and retrieval APIs for current unchanged code
- **Consumes:** repo contents from `src/jobs/workspace.ts`, embedding provider/runtime configuration, PostgreSQL/pgvector storage
- **Provides to downstream milestones:** truthful current-code chunks with commit/ref provenance plus semantic retrieval over unchanged code
- **Does not own:** historical PR diff-hunk corpus, graph substrate from M040, or prompt consumption logic in M038

milestones.title: Contributor Tier Truthfulness
milestones.vision: Fix Kodiai's contributor-experience classification so a clearly established contributor is not labeled as a newcomer in review output, using the CrystalP `xbmc/xbmc#28132` repro to correct stored tier advancement, review-surface author labeling, and cache/fallback consistency the right way.
success_criteria: A clearly experienced contributor is no longer labeled with newcomer-style author context in the review path.
Stored contributor profile tiers advance truthfully as contributor activity accumulates.
Review output, cache reuse, and fallback classification no longer contradict each other about contributor experience.
The real repro on `xbmc/xbmc#28132` is mechanically covered by regression verification.

key_risks.risk: Stored contributor tiers may be structurally stuck because score updates do not trigger recalculation.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If true, review output will stay wrong for any contributor whose profile was first created with a low/default tier.

key_risks.risk: Two author-tier systems with different shapes may create contradictory behavior even after the stored-tier bug is fixed.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The review path can still regress through author cache or lightweight fallback classification if precedence is unclear.

key_risks.risk: The repro may involve undercounted activity signals, not just stale state.
key_risks.whyItMatters: A narrow recalculation fix would be incomplete if the underlying scoring inputs systematically underclassify active contributors.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Stored contributor tiers may be structurally stuck because score updates do not trigger recalculation.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The CrystalP-shaped repro can be reproduced and the stored contributor profile can advance out of the incorrect low tier under corrected tier-state logic.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Two author-tier systems with different shapes may create contradictory behavior even after the stored-tier bug is fixed.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The review handler and prompt/output path consume the corrected contributor tier source consistently for the repro case.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The repro may involve undercounted activity signals, not just stale state.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Cache-hit, cache-miss, fallback, and adjacent contributor-history scenarios preserve truthful labeling instead of silently reintroducing stale or contradictory states.

milestones.verification_contract: Focused unit and integration tests prove truthful stored-tier advancement, review-path precedence, and repro regression coverage.
milestones.verification_integration: Exercise the real review classification path (`src/handlers/review.ts` → prompt/output behavior) using the CrystalP-shaped repro and adjacent contributor-history cases.
milestones.verification_operational: Verify cache-hit, cache-miss, and degraded-enrichment paths do not reintroduce stale contributor labels under fail-open execution.
milestones.verification_uat: Human can inspect the repro case expectations and confirm that an obviously experienced contributor is no longer described as a newcomer in review output.
definition_of_done: The CrystalP repro path for `xbmc/xbmc#28132` no longer produces newcomer-style author context.
Stored contributor profiles can advance truthfully when score/history warrants it.
Review output consumes the corrected contributor tier source consistently.
Cache and fallback classification no longer preserve contradictory contributor labels once truthful profile state exists.
Regression verification covers the real repro and adjacent contributor-history cases.
All milestone success criteria are re-checked against real code paths before closure.
milestones.requirement_coverage:
Covers: R039, R040, R041, R042
Leaves for later: R043
Out of scope for this milestone: R044, R045
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- `src/contributor/*` tier-advancement fix or equivalent shared classification primitive that turns updated contributor activity into a truthful stored tier
- repro fixture / verification helper for the CrystalP `xbmc#28132` misclassification path
- explicit normalization rule for mapping stored contributor tiers into review-path author-context behavior

Consumes:
- existing contributor profile store, expertise scoring, and review-handler author classification entrypoint

### S02 → S03
Produces:
- review-path wiring that consumes the corrected contributor tier source of truth in `src/handlers/review.ts`
- prompt/output regression coverage proving the repro no longer emits newcomer-style author context
- documented precedence between contributor profile state and fallback classification

Consumes from S01:
- truthful stored-tier advancement behavior
- repro verification helper / fixtures
- tier normalization rule

### S01 → S03
Produces:
- reproducible contributor-state scenarios that exercise stale/stuck tier behavior and corrected advancement behavior

Consumes:
- existing contributor profile and cache infrastructure

milestones.title: Restore Mention Review Publication and Reverify PR #80
milestones.vision: Restore Kodiai's end-to-end `@kodiai review` behavior in production so explicit PR mentions execute, publish a truthful GitHub outcome exactly once, and remain intact while PR #80 is synced, cleaned up, and re-verified.
success_criteria: A live explicit `@kodiai review` request on xbmc/kodiai PR #80 produces a visible GitHub outcome in production instead of completing silently.
Production logs and result surfaces identify whether publication was attempted, skipped by idempotency, or rejected by GitHub via reviewOutputKey and publish-stage diagnostics.
Deployment and runbook steps preserve the ACA mention-review wiring needed for live verification and do not silently strip required app configuration.
The cleaned PR #80 branch carries the production hotfixes, remaining concrete review feedback is addressed, and targeted verification passes without regressing the repaired production path.

key_risks.risk: The explicit mention review path may still be missing or bypassing the deterministic publish branch, or idempotency may be falsely suppressing publication.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the code path is wrong or the idempotency check is over-matching, Kodiai can keep succeeding in ACA while publishing nothing on GitHub.

key_risks.risk: Publish failures may still be swallowed by shared error-comment/update helpers or generic wrappers, leaving production runs ambiguous.
key_risks.whyItMatters: A second silent failure would force another live forensic pass instead of making the next failure attributable from logs and PR-visible output.

key_risks.risk: Cherry-picking the production fix stack onto PR #80 and finishing review cleanup could re-break the repaired mention-review path or destabilize the branch.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The user explicitly wants the public PR cleaned up after production is fixed, but not at the cost of regressing the just-restored bot behavior.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Whether explicit mention reviews reach the real publish branch or are blocked by routing/idempotency/API failure
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A live explicit PR mention review reaches the repaired publish path and produces a GitHub-visible outcome with attributable logs instead of silent success.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Whether publish failures and deploy drift remain too ambiguous to diagnose quickly
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Failed or skipped publication states emit exact diagnostics, and deploy verification preserves the ACA env wiring and runtime contract needed for mention reviews.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Whether syncing mainline hotfixes and remaining PR fixes onto PR #80 will regress the repaired production behavior
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The cleaned PR branch passes its targeted verification suite and a fresh live production mention review still publishes correctly after the branch work is complete.

milestones.verification_contract: Focused regression tests around mention.ts, review-idempotency, shared error publication, and any new publish-path helpers; targeted branch verifiers for the remaining PR #80 fixes.
milestones.verification_integration: Exercise the real webhook -> mention handler -> ACA executor -> GitHub publication path against xbmc/kodiai PR #80 and confirm the resulting review/comment state on GitHub.
milestones.verification_operational: Deploy the repaired app to main, verify health/readiness, inspect structured logs around reviewOutputKey/idempotency/publish outcomes, and confirm deploy.sh/runbook steps preserve required ACA wiring.
milestones.verification_uat: Confirm an explicit `@kodiai review` on PR #80 produces exactly one visible Kodiai outcome, not a reaction-only acknowledgment or silent success, and confirm the cleaned PR branch remains reviewable and verified.
definition_of_done: Production mention-triggered PR review publication works again end-to-end on the live deployment.
No explicit PR mention review can finish as a silent success; failed publication paths are visible in logs and/or user-facing fallback output.
The production deploy/runbook contract for ACA mention reviews is documented, rerunnable, and matches the live behavior used to prove the fix.
The PR #80 branch contains the required production hotfixes, excludes `.gsd`/runtime noise, and has the remaining concrete review feedback addressed.
Targeted tests/verifiers pass for all touched subsystems, and the production mention-review path is rechecked after the PR cleanup work.
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: R043, R044. Partially covers: R011 where deployment/runbook docs are updated to match the repaired mention-review production path. Leaves for later: broader handler decomposition remains under deferred R017. Orphan risks: none.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 -> S02

Produces:
- A repaired explicit-mention publish contract in `src/handlers/mention.ts` with attributable `reviewOutputKey`, idempotency, and publish-attempt logging
- A live production proof point on PR #80 showing the repaired path produces a GitHub-visible outcome instead of silent success

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02 -> S03

Produces:
- Stable failure diagnostics for publish skip/reject/error cases
- A deploy-safe ACA mention-review wiring contract (`ACA_JOB_*`, `MCP_INTERNAL_BASE_URL`, safe app update behavior) plus rerunnable docs/runbook steps

Consumes:
- S01 live publish repair and its logging surfaces

### S03 -> S04

Produces:
- A synced `origin/pr/multi-m035-m042-clean` branch carrying the production hotfix stack without `.gsd`, `.tmp`, or other runtime artifacts
- A clean git/history surface for finishing the remaining PR review feedback

Consumes:
- S02 deployment/publish contract and the repaired hotfix commits from main

### S04 -> S05

Produces:
- Remaining concrete PR #80 review fixes implemented on top of the synced hotfix stack
- Targeted verification results proving the branch cleanup did not regress mention-review publication code paths

Consumes:
- S03 synced PR branch and S02's stabilized production contract

milestones.title: Audit Recent XBMC Review Correctness
milestones.vision: Ship a real operator audit surface for the recent `xbmc/xbmc` Kodiai review stream across both automatic PR review and explicit `@kodiai review` lanes. The milestone should let operators select a deterministic recent sample, classify each PR with evidence-backed verdicts instead of guessing from approvals alone, repair the first real production defect or missing-truth gap the audit exposes, and finish with one rerunnable verifier/runbook entrypoint that can be used later without manual PR archaeology.
success_criteria: A deterministic recent sample of approximately twelve `xbmc/xbmc` Kodiai-reviewed PRs can be selected across both automatic and explicit review lanes using documented rules rather than manual scrolling.
Each sampled PR receives a truthful verdict backed by GitHub-visible output plus internal publication evidence, with explicit `indeterminate` handling when access or evidence is missing.
Valid clean approvals remain classified as healthy, while at least one failure-shaped path stays distinguishable from clean approval by code, tests, and the verifier.
Operators can rerun one documented audit command later and recover the same selection rule, verdict taxonomy, and evidence drill-down path without rediscovering the method.

key_risks.risk: Automatic clean approval plus standalone Review Details is a legitimate shipped outcome, so a naive audit could generate false failures and destroy operator trust.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The milestone only helps if it separates valid clean reviews from missing publication; treating approval-only shapes as broken by default would make the final audit noisy and unusable.

key_risks.risk: Explicit `@kodiai review` truth is richer but currently more log-centric than the automatic lane, so recent-window repeatability may fail if durable evidence is missing or log access is inconsistent.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the verifier cannot reliably correlate explicit-lane publication outcomes after the fact, S02 may need observability/durability work before the audit can be considered trustworthy.

key_risks.risk: A recent-dozen selector can drift or starve one lane if it is based on ad-hoc GitHub search text instead of real review markers and explicit fill rules.
key_risks.whyItMatters: The milestone promises a repeatable cross-lane audit. If the sample rule is not deterministic and balanced enough, operators will not be able to compare reruns or trust coverage.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Whether recent live `xbmc/xbmc` reviews can be selected and correlated inward without heuristic archaeology.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A real marker-based, lane-aware sample can be collected from GitHub-visible Kodiai output and matched to current internal evidence strongly enough to emit truthful provisional verdicts.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Whether the first ambiguity the audit exposes is a product bug, a missing durable publish-truth seam, or merely an expected clean-review shape.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The smallest real gap exposed by S01 is repaired at the root cause, regression-covered, and shown by a live audit rerun to no longer produce the prior false or ambiguous outcome.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Whether the audit can be rerun later by operators without rediscovering selection rules, access prerequisites, or verdict meanings.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A stable verifier command plus runbook can execute the recent-review audit end to end, surface preflight/access truthfully, and emit machine-readable per-PR evidence and verdicts.

milestones.verification_contract: Every slice must leave behind executable proof. S01 and S03 must exercise real `xbmc/xbmc` PR history through GitHub review/comment surfaces; S02 must rerun the exact failure or ambiguity it fixes. Local tests can prove parsing, classification, and persistence seams, but milestone completion also requires one live recent-sample audit that depends on real GitHub history and current publication evidence rather than simulation.
milestones.verification_integration: Verify the assembled flow across four boundaries: GitHub reviews/comments -> `reviewOutputKey` parsing and sample selection -> lane-specific internal evidence resolvers (automatic DB-backed evidence, explicit publish-resolution/log evidence) -> operator CLI/report output. Completion requires matching repo/PR identities across those boundaries and truthful handling of missing evidence.
milestones.verification_operational: Run the final verifier in operator mode with real environment access. Confirm preflight explicitly reports missing GitHub, DB, or Azure evidence access instead of silently omitting checks; confirm rerunning without code changes preserves the same documented sample rule and verdict taxonomy.
milestones.verification_uat: An operator can run the documented audit command, see which PRs were sampled, inspect why a clean approval is valid versus suspicious, and follow the runbook to drill into one flagged PR without reading source code or manually scrolling GitHub history.
definition_of_done: M044 ships a real recent-review audit/verifier surface, not only a one-off report or log query.
R045 has an explicit slice-level path: S01 establishes evidence correlation, S02 closes the first real gap exposed by the audit, and S03 packages the rerunnable operator surface.
At least one failure-shaped path remains distinguishable from clean approval by the shipped code, tests, and verifier output.
Any real defect surfaced by the audit is either fixed at the root cause or truthfully preserved as `indeterminate` because required evidence is unavailable.
Docs explain rerun prerequisites, verdict meanings, and evidence drill-down steps for flagged PRs.
milestones.requirement_coverage: Primary requirement: R045. Planned ownership is S03 as the primary operator surface, with S01 supplying deterministic sample selection and evidence correlation and S02 closing the first defect or missing-durability gap exposed by the audit. Active prerequisite continuity requirements R043 and R044 remain owned by M043 and are consumed here as fixed contracts that must not regress, not reopened product scope.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **GitHub-visible output boundary**
  - Authoritative sources: PR reviews, issue comments, and review comments carrying Kodiai markers / Review Details.
  - S01 owns collection and normalization; S03 owns operator-facing rendering of the resulting evidence.
- **Review-output identity boundary**
  - Contract: `reviewOutputKey` marker format, parsed repo/PR/action/delivery/head identity, and retry-suffix normalization.
  - S01 establishes the parser/correlation seam; S02 hardens it if audit drift or retry ambiguity is exposed.
- **Internal publication-evidence boundary**
  - Automatic lane: durable `reviews` / `findings` / checkpoint / telemetry evidence.
  - Explicit lane: idempotency and `publishResolution` evidence, currently log-heavy and potentially needing durability hardening in S02.
- **Operator verification boundary**
  - Contract: package script / CLI entrypoint, human + JSON output, stable verdict taxonomy, and truthful preflight/access reporting.
  - S03 owns the final operator surface and runbook.

milestones.title: Contributor Experience Product Contract and Architecture
milestones.vision: Turn contributor experience from mixed tier strings and surface-specific heuristics into one explicit product contract that governs GitHub review behavior, Slack profile semantics, retrieval shaping, and opt-out handling through a single coherent architecture.
success_criteria: GitHub review prompt and Review Details consume one explicit contributor-experience contract instead of raw mixed taxonomy strings.
Slack profile, opt-in/out behavior, and retrieval shaping either follow the same contract or are intentionally removed from it with matching code and proof.
Opt-out and degraded/no-signal paths are truthful and intentionally constrained rather than silently falling back to contradictory adaptation.
A rerunnable operator verification surface proves cross-surface coherence and guards against future drift.

key_risks.risk: The correct product answer may be to narrow contributor-experience adaptation, so premature taxonomy unification could hard-code the wrong behavior.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the roadmap optimizes for preserving every current tier surface, the milestone can ship cleaner code while still expressing the wrong product contract.

key_risks.risk: Fallback/cache labels can overclaim certainty if they are treated as equivalent to profile-backed contributor signals.
key_risks.whyItMatters: This would recreate the M042-style truthfulness problem under a different vocabulary and make Review Details or Slack output misleading.

key_risks.risk: Slack, retrieval, and operator surfaces can continue drifting even if the GitHub review path is corrected.
key_risks.whyItMatters: R046 is about cross-surface coherence, so local fixes without shared verification would still leave the milestone incomplete.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: What contributor experience is actually allowed to change on the core GitHub review surface is still implicit.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A typed contract drives review prompt behavior and Review Details for profile-backed, fallback, unknown, and degraded scenarios without contradictory vocabulary.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Opt-out, Slack output, and retrieval shaping currently make or imply conflicting product promises.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: These surfaces either consume the same contract or are explicitly removed from it, and opt-out behavior becomes truthful in both code and copy.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: The milestone currently lacks one integrated proof surface that catches future drift across consumers.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: An operator verifier command reports named checks for review, Slack, retrieval, and opt-out behavior from one rerunnable entrypoint.

milestones.verification_contract: Primary contract proof comes from exercising the real review-path inputs that drive prompt shaping and Review Details across profile-backed, coarse fallback, unknown, and opted-out or degraded author scenarios, with assertions against explicit policy projections rather than raw tier strings.
milestones.verification_integration: Integration verification must cover Slack `/kodiai profile` and opt-in/out flows, review-time contributor resolution independent of `knowledgeStore` gating, and retrieval query shaping or exclusion decisions using the same contract.
milestones.verification_operational: An operator command must emit named pass/fail checks and JSON output that expose cross-surface contract drift without requiring code inspection, and the rerun procedure must be documented.
milestones.verification_uat: A maintainer should be able to inspect contributor-experience behavior on the review surface and Slack profile surface without seeing contradictory tier vocabularies, unclear opt-out semantics, or unexplained adaptation on degraded paths.
definition_of_done: An explicit typed contract or equivalent policy surface exists and is the sole owner of contributor-experience projections.
GitHub review prompt and Review Details no longer duplicate or independently reinterpret raw contributor tier strings.
Slack, retrieval, and identity/opt-out surfaces match the shipped contract or are intentionally removed with code and verification reflecting that choice.
Opt-out behavior and degraded paths are truthful and test-covered.
A rerunnable verifier and supporting documentation prove cross-surface coherence.
milestones.requirement_coverage:
- **R046 (active)** — primary owner: **S01**; supporting slices: **S02, S03**. S01 defines the contract on the core GitHub review surface, S02 applies it across Slack/opt-out/retrieval surfaces, and S03 ships the integrated operator verification surface that proves cross-surface coherence.
- **Continuity constraints from validated R039-R042** — preserved as non-regression expectations across S01-S03: profile-backed truth beats weaker signals, degraded paths remain truthful, and contradictory guidance is not reintroduced.
- **Downstream continuity for R048** — S03 leaves one verification surface future rollout and calibration work can reuse instead of rediscovering contract boundaries.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### Boundary map
- **Signal sources:** `contributorProfileStore`, `author_cache`, fallback classifier, and contributor opt-out state.
- **Contract seam:** one typed contributor-experience contract resolves provenance and confidence into allowed behavior, visible wording, and optional retrieval hints.
- **Primary consumer:** `src/handlers/review.ts` plus `src/execution/review-prompt.ts` and `src/lib/review-utils.ts`.
- **Secondary consumers:** `src/slack/slash-command-handler.ts`, `src/handlers/identity-suggest.ts`, `src/knowledge/multi-query-retrieval.ts`, and legacy `src/knowledge/retrieval-query.ts` (align or retire).
- **Proof and operations:** milestone verifier script, tests, and runbook expose contract drift across all in-scope consumers.

milestones.title: Contributor Tier Calibration and Fixture Audit
milestones.vision: Turn contributor-tier calibration for `xbmc/xbmc` into a repeatable product proof: checked-in contributor fixtures with explicit provenance, a verifier that measures the current live incremental path against the intended full-signal model, and a final keep/retune/replace verdict that tells M047 exactly what must change.
success_criteria: A reusable xbmc contributor fixture set exists in-repo with explicit provenance and exclusion rules.
Kodiai can rerun calibration against the fixture set and compare the actual live incremental path with the intended full-signal path.
The milestone produces an explicit keep/retune/replace verdict and a concrete M047 change contract, not an open-ended research note.

key_risks.risk: Fixture truth may be contaminated by bot identities, alias fragmentation, or insufficient middle-band coverage.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the input corpus is not trustworthy, the model can appear wrong or right for the wrong reason and M047 will optimize against noise.

key_risks.risk: The intended scoring model and the currently wired runtime path may diverge materially.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Certifying only the intended model would leave Kodiai shipping a different behavior than the one the calibration report claims is sound.

key_risks.risk: Percentile tiers may remain unstable on small or tied cohorts even with better fixtures.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If instability is structural, cutoff retuning alone will not create believable contributor tiers under the M045 contract.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Can Kodiai build a truthful xbmc contributor corpus before tuning anything?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Checked-in fixtures, provenance metadata, exclusion reasons, and refresh/validation commands prove the calibration inputs are deliberate and reusable.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Does the current live path behave plausibly under the M045 contributor contract, or is the architecture itself misaligned?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: A repeatable evaluator will show actual live incremental behavior beside the intended full-signal model, including cohort-instability and freshness edge cases.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Will the milestone end with an actionable decision instead of another ambiguous audit?
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The integrated M046 report will emit an explicit keep/retune/replace verdict and name the exact M047 change targets required.

milestones.verification_contract: Verification for M046 is report-first and evidence-backed: each slice must ship a runnable verifier surface, targeted Bun tests around any new helpers or edge-case logic, and deterministic JSON output that can be inspected independently of prose conclusions.
milestones.verification_integration: S01 must prove the fixture refresh/validation seam through existing GitHub App patterns and offline snapshot validation. S02 must prove the scorer, tier calculator, profile freshness semantics, and M045 contract seam can be exercised together on the same fixtures. S03 must prove the top-level package script composes lower-level outputs into one explicit milestone verdict.
milestones.verification_operational: All verifier entrypoints should support human-readable output plus JSON, degrade clearly when live GitHub access is unavailable, and preserve offline deterministic reruns from checked-in fixture data so operators can distinguish access problems from model problems.
milestones.verification_uat: An operator can inspect the checked-in xbmc fixture set, run the calibration scripts locally, see contributor-by-contributor outcomes for both model paths, and read one final report that plainly states whether Kodiai should keep, retune, or replace its contributor-tier mechanism.
definition_of_done: Fixture truth, calibration evaluation, and final verdict surfaces are all runnable through real package or script entrypoints.
The milestone can explain contributor-tier plausibility using explicit xbmc evidence rather than hand-picked anecdotes.
Requirement R047 has a clear path from fixture truth set to evaluation harness to explicit verdict artifact.
Live-access gaps are reported as degraded states instead of being mistaken for model success or failure.
milestones.requirement_coverage:
- **R047** — fully mapped for M046. **Primary owner:** S02, which evaluates the scoring/tiering mechanism against checked-in xbmc fixtures and the M045 contract. **Supporting slices:** S01 provides the trusted fixture truth set and provenance surface; S03 converts the evidence into the explicit keep/retune/replace verdict and M047 change contract.
- No additional M046-specific active requirements were identified in the preloaded context beyond R047.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
- **GitHub App auth + Octokit live sampling** (`src/auth/github-app.ts`, existing verifier patterns) → **S01** refreshes fixture data when credentials are present.
- **Checked-in xbmc calibration fixtures** (new fixture/report artifacts under repo control) → **S01 provides** the deterministic truth set consumed by **S02** and **S03**.
- **Contributor scoring + tiering modules** (`src/contributor/expertise-scorer.ts`, `src/contributor/tier-calculator.ts`, `src/contributor/profile-store.ts`) → **S02** evaluates actual runtime behavior, intended full-signal behavior, and instability edges.
- **Contributor-experience contract seam from M045** (`src/contributor/experience-contract.ts`, downstream review/slack surfaces) → **S02** interprets calibration impact; **S03** states the final product verdict in contract terms.
- **Top-level verification/report entrypoints** (`scripts/verify-m046-*.ts`, `package.json`) → **S03** publishes the integrated milestone verdict surface.

milestones.title: Contributor Experience Redesign and Calibration Rollout
milestones.vision: Turn the M045 contributor-experience contract and M046 replace verdict into shipped behavior by making persisted contributor state trustworthy, rolling that shared resolution through GitHub review, Slack, retrieval, and profile-plumbing surfaces, and proving cross-surface coherence end to end.
success_criteria: GitHub review behavior, Review Details, retrieval query shaping, and Slack profile output all resolve from one shipped contributor model rather than mixed raw-tier semantics.
Linked-but-unscored and legacy/pre-M047 persisted profiles fail open truthfully instead of surfacing `profile-backed` newcomer guidance.

The replacement calibrated/full-signal producer path yields the M046-expected outcomes for the retained fixture set while preserving explicit degradation and freshness reporting when evidence is stale or incomplete.
A canonical `verify:m047` proof surface demonstrates cross-surface coherence and the absence of the old mixed-taxonomy regression.

key_risks.risk: Persisted contributor rows may continue to collapse linked, legacy, and calibrated states into the same outward newcomer semantics.
key_risks.whyItMatters: If the trust boundary stays ambiguous, every downstream surface can look coherent while still being wrong.

key_risks.risk: Replacing the `pr_authored`-only live producer may still happen too late in the request lifecycle to affect the review being published.
key_risks.whyItMatters: A post-review recalculation would preserve the current blind spot where the shipped review uses stale or placeholder contributor truth.

key_risks.risk: Slack/profile and retrieval consumers may drift if the rollout fixes only the review resolver.
key_risks.whyItMatters: R048 fails if one surface keeps reading raw stored tiers while another surface uses the new contract.

key_risks.risk: The final verifier could accidentally re-use helper logic and miss real source-resolution regressions.
key_risks.whyItMatters: Copy-level proof is not enough for this milestone; the operator surface must catch runtime truth-source mistakes.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Persisted contributor rows do not currently encode trust or rollout version, so linked or legacy rows can masquerade as scored newcomer guidance.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: The live review resolver can distinguish linked-unscored, legacy, calibrated, opted-out, and degraded states before publishing prompt or Review Details behavior.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Downstream Slack and retrieval surfaces may keep consuming raw stored tiers even after review resolution is fixed.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Slack/profile continuity and retrieval hints use the same contract/resolution outputs as the GitHub review surface with no raw-tier leakage.

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Existing contract renderers can stay green while runtime source resolution is still wrong.
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: An operator-facing integrated verifier catches source-resolution regressions using real stored-profile, cache, and fallback scenarios while preserving nested M045/M046 evidence.

milestones.verification_contract: Contract proof for M047 must exercise real stored contributor states and resolver logic, not only direct projections from `experience-contract.ts`. Slice completion requires mechanical evidence that persisted state -> contract -> surface behavior stays truthful across trusted, unscored, legacy, degraded, opt-out, and coarse-fallback paths.
milestones.verification_integration: Milestone closeout requires one scenario matrix to reach the shipped review prompt, Review Details, retrieval hint shaping, Slack/profile output, and contributor-profile plumbing without raw-tier drift. `verify:m047` should compose the upstream M045 and M046 proof surfaces rather than replacing them.
milestones.verification_operational: The rollout must preserve fail-open behavior when contributor data is missing, stale, malformed, or not yet recalibrated, and it must emit named diagnostics for legacy, unscored, degraded, and fallback resolution paths.
milestones.verification_uat: Exercise representative scenarios for linked-but-unscored, calibrated retained contributor, stale or degraded contributor, opt-out, and coarse-fallback cache states. Confirm that the observable GitHub review surfaces, Slack profile output, and retrieval hint behavior all match the same contributor-experience contract.
definition_of_done: No in-scope tier-related surface treats a merely linked or legacy profile row as trustworthy `profile-backed` guidance.
GitHub review, Review Details, retrieval hints, Slack profile output, and continuity messaging all consume one contributor-state interpretation.
`verify:m047`, its targeted tests, and the relevant existing verifier suites pass with human and JSON outputs aligned.
Active requirements relevant to this milestone are explicitly mapped to owning/supporting slices and the milestone decisions are recorded in `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`.
milestones.requirement_coverage:
- R046 (active): supported by S01, S02, and S03. S01 re-establishes truthful review-time resolution on the M045 contract, S02 extends the same contract across Slack/retrieval/profile continuity surfaces, and S03 proves the shipped surfaces reflect that contract coherently.
- R048 (active): primary closeout requirement for this milestone. S01 fixes producer truth and persisted trust semantics, S02 rolls that truth through every remaining live consumer surface, and S03 provides the end-to-end runtime coherence proof required for validation.
- No additional active requirements are directly expanded by M047; previously validated M042/M045/M046 contributor requirements remain continuity constraints exercised by slice verification rather than reopened scope.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
| Boundary | Stable interface | What changes in M047 | Closed by |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Persisted contributor state | `contributor_profiles` + profile-store API | Add trust/version semantics that distinguish linked-unscored, legacy, calibrated, and degraded rows | S01 |
| Review-time resolution | `resolveAuthorTier()` -> `ContributorExperienceContract` | Stop trusting raw stored tiers as truth; make the review entrypoint consume trustworthy calibrated state or fail open | S01 |
| Shared contract seam | `src/contributor/experience-contract.ts` projections | Keep M045 vocabulary stable while feeding it better source-resolution inputs | S01, S02 |
| Downstream consumers | Slack profile/link/opt flows and retrieval hints | Remove raw-tier consumption and align every consumer with the shared contract/resolution seam | S02 |
| Operator proof surface | milestone verifier commands | Add `verify:m047` that composes M045/M046 proof with real runtime source-resolution scenarios | S03 |

slices.title: Webhook Foundation

# S01: Handler scaffold and repo detection

**Created the addon-check handler scaffold with addonRepos config field, PR file inspection, and addon ID extraction — plus fixed 53 pre-existing TypeScript errors to achieve clean tsc exit 0.**

## What Happened

S01 delivered the full handler scaffold for M030's addon rule enforcement feature. T01 added `addonRepos` to `AppConfig` (Zod schema with comma-split transform, default of three xbmc repos), created `src/handlers/addon-check.ts` following the `createIssueOpenedHandler` factory pattern, and wrote 5 unit tests covering all scenarios. The handler registers on `pull_request.opened` and `pull_request.synchronize`, gates on `config.addonRepos.includes(repo)`, calls `octokit.rest.pulls.listFiles`, extracts first path segments from files containing a slash (excluding root-level files like README.md), deduplicates, sorts, and logs the addon IDs at info level with structured bindings. T02 wired the handler into `src/index.ts` (unconditionally, after existing handler registrations) and fixed 10 AppConfig stub objects in test/script files that gained a required `addonRepos` field.

The verification gate required `bun run tsc --noEmit` to exit 0. T02 had reduced errors from 68 to 56 (12 fewer, all pre-existing). The gate still failed with exit code 2 because pre-existing errors existed. This required fixing all 53 remaining errors across the codebase: (1) `EmbeddingRepairCorpus` widening with `as readonly string[]` for `.includes()` calls; (2) non-null assertions on optional repair methods in `scripts/embedding-repair.ts`; (3) `EmbeddingRepairCheckpoint | null` parameter type fix in `normalizeStatusReport`; (4) `as const satisfies` for `REPAIR_CORPUS` literals to narrow from `EmbeddingRepairCorpus` to the specific literal; (5) `listRepairCandidates` interface types updated to `RepairCandidateRow[]` in `IssueStore`, `LearningMemoryStore`, `ReviewCommentStore`, `CodeSnippetStore` interfaces; (6) `rowToRepairCandidate` rewritten to return `RepairCandidateRow` directly (snake_case shape); (7) wiki test mock stubs updated to include the 4 new required `WikiPageStore` methods; (8) `createScopedRepairStore` destructure-after-guard pattern to resolve optional method closures; (9) `TransactionSql → as unknown as Sql` in `embedding-audit.ts`; (10) `vectorDistance: number | null` alignment in `retriever-verifier.ts`; (11) module cast double-cast pattern (`as unknown as ModuleType`) for M027 test type mismatches; (12) `RepairStore` exported from `wiki-embedding-repair.ts`; (13) `normalizeCheckpoint` parameter union with `null`; and (14) non-null assertions for array index access in M027 tests. After all fixes, `bun run tsc --noEmit` exits 0 cleanly.

## Verification

bun test src/handlers/addon-check.test.ts — 5 pass, 0 fail. bun run tsc --noEmit — exit 0, no errors.

## Requirements Advanced

None.

## Requirements Validated

None.

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

T02 description said the pre-existing baseline was 68 errors and after fixes would be 56. The verification gate requires exit 0 (not just 'no new errors'). This required fixing all 53 remaining pre-existing errors across embedding-repair, wiki-embedding-repair, audit, retrieval, and M027 test infrastructure — none of which are M030 code.

## Known Limitations

None.

## Follow-ups

None.

- `src/config.ts` — Added addonRepos Zod field with comma-split transform and default of three xbmc repos
- `src/handlers/addon-check.ts` — New addon-check handler factory: registers on pull_request.opened/synchronize, gates on addonRepos, extracts addon IDs from listFiles output
- `src/handlers/addon-check.test.ts` — 5 unit tests covering registration, repo gating, ID extraction, empty PR, root-level file exclusion
- `src/index.ts` — Imports and calls createAddonCheckHandler unconditionally after existing handlers

# S01 UAT: Handler scaffold and repo detection

## Preconditions
- App is running with default `ADDON_REPOS` (xbmc/repo-plugins, xbmc/repo-scripts, xbmc/repo-scrapers)
- GitHub App installation exists for xbmc org

## Test Cases

### TC-01: Handler registers on both pull_request events
**What:** `createAddonCheckHandler` registers handlers for both `pull_request.opened` and `pull_request.synchronize`.
**How:** Inspect the EventRouter after calling `createAddonCheckHandler`. Both event keys must be present.
**Expected:** Router has entries for both `pull_request.opened` and `pull_request.synchronize`.
**Evidence:** Unit test `registers on pull_request.opened and pull_request.synchronize` — passes.

### TC-02: Non-addon repo produces no output
**What:** A PR on a repo not in `addonRepos` (e.g., `xbmc/xbmc`) triggers the handler but exits early without calling `listFiles`.
**How:** Trigger with `repository.full_name = "xbmc/xbmc"`, config `addonRepos = ["xbmc/repo-plugins"]`.
**Expected:** `listFiles` is never called. No info log emitted. Debug log confirms repo not in addonRepos.
**Evidence:** Unit test `non-addon repo returns without calling listFiles` — passes.

### TC-03: Addon repo logs correct addon IDs (sorted, deduplicated)
**What:** A PR on `xbmc/repo-plugins` with files from two addons logs both addon IDs sorted alphabetically.
**How:** Files: `["plugin.video.foo/addon.xml", "plugin.video.foo/icon.png", "plugin.audio.bar/addon.xml"]`.
**Expected:** `logger.info` called with `addonIds: ["plugin.audio.bar", "plugin.video.foo"]` (sorted, deduplicated).
**Evidence:** Unit test `addon repo logs correct addon IDs` — passes.

### TC-04: Empty PR logs empty addon ID list
**What:** A PR with zero changed files logs an empty array (not an error).
**How:** Files: `[]`.
**Expected:** `logger.info` called with `addonIds: []`.
**Evidence:** Unit test `empty PR (no files) logs empty addon ID list` — passes.

### TC-05: Root-level files are excluded
**What:** Root-level files (no `/` in path) like `README.md` are excluded; files with a `/` (even `.github/`) are treated as having an addon directory first segment.
**How:** Files: `["README.md", "plugin.video.foo/addon.xml"]`.
**Expected:** `addonIds: ["plugin.video.foo"]` — `README.md` excluded because no slash.
**Evidence:** Unit test `root-level files (no slash) are excluded` — passes.

### TC-06: addonRepos default config
**What:** When `ADDON_REPOS` env var is not set, `config.addonRepos` defaults to the three standard xbmc repos.
**How:** Parse config with no `ADDON_REPOS` env var set.
**Expected:** `config.addonRepos` equals `["xbmc/repo-plugins", "xbmc/repo-scripts", "xbmc/repo-scrapers"]`.

### TC-07: TypeScript clean compilation
**What:** Adding `addonRepos` to `AppConfig` must not break any existing TypeScript consumers.
**How:** Run `bun run tsc --noEmit`.
**Expected:** Exit code 0, no errors.
**Evidence:** tsc --noEmit exits 0.

### TC-08: Handler wired into production entrypoint
**What:** `createAddonCheckHandler` is called in `src/index.ts` so the handler is active in the running app.
**How:** Grep `src/index.ts` for `createAddonCheckHandler`.
**Expected:** Import and unconditional call are present.
**Evidence:** T02 implementation confirmed — handler registered after existing handlers, outside optional store guards.

# S02: kodi-addon-checker subprocess and output parsing

**Built the addon-checker subprocess runner and output parser, wired them into the addon-check handler with workspace lifecycle and job queue, producing structured findings — 30/30 tests pass, TypeScript clean.**

## What Happened

S02 delivered two interdependent units of work across T01 and T02.

**T01 — Pure runner module**

`src/lib/addon-checker-runner.ts` was built as a pure injectable module following the `usage-analyzer.ts` pattern established in M027. Key design points:
- `ValidKodiVersions` is a `readonly string[]` of the 10 known Kodi release branch names (nexus, omega, matrix, leia, jarvis, isengard, helix, gotham, frodo, dharma).
- `parseCheckerOutput(raw, addonId)` strips ANSI codes with `/\x1B\[[0-9;]*m/g` before line splitting, then matches `^(ERROR|WARN|INFO): (.+)$` per line; non-matching lines are silently dropped.
- `resolveCheckerBranch(baseBranch)` returns the branch if present in `ValidKodiVersions`, else null.
- `runAddonChecker` spawns `kodi-addon-checker --branch <branch> <addonDir>`, captures stdout, and parses it. ENOENT → `toolNotFound: true`. Timeout → `timedOut: true`. Non-zero exit code is NOT an error (the tool exits 1 when findings exist). Non-ENOENT errors fail open (returns empty findings).
- `withTimeBudget` was reused from `usage-analyzer.ts` rather than duplicated.
- `addonId` is derived from the last path segment of `addonDir` inside `runAddonChecker`.
- The `__runSubprocessForTests` injection point matches `__runGrepForTests` shape exactly.
- 19 tests covering all three functions across all specified edge cases.

**T02 — Handler wiring**

`src/handlers/addon-check.ts` was updated to accept `workspaceManager` and `jobQueue` as deps alongside the existing `listFiles`. The full flow:
1. `resolveCheckerBranch(payload.pull_request.base.ref)` — unknown branch → warn and return.
2. Empty `addonIds` → early return before `enqueue` to avoid no-op workspace creation.
3. `jobQueue.enqueue(installationId, async () => { ... })` wraps all workspace work.
4. Inside the job: `workspaceManager.create(headBranch)` → for each addonId call `runAddonChecker` → log each finding (`addonId`, `level`, `message`), log summary (`addonIds`, `totalFindings`), `workspace.cleanup()` in finally.
5. `src/index.ts` updated to pass `workspaceManager` and `jobQueue` to `createAddonCheckHandler`.

Test decisions:
- `createMockLoggerWithArrays()` centralizes shared-array writes so child logger assertions work without `.mock.calls` traversal.
- Cleanup-on-throw test throws from `workspace.create` (not the subprocess) since `runAddonChecker` fails open on non-ENOENT errors.
- 11 handler tests (5 from scaffold + 6 new) all pass.

All three verification gates pass: 19 runner tests, 11 handler tests, `bun run tsc --noEmit` exits 0.

## Verification

Three-gate verification all pass:
1. `bun test src/lib/addon-checker-runner.test.ts` → 19 pass, 0 fail (55 expect() calls)
2. `bun test src/handlers/addon-check.test.ts` → 11 pass, 0 fail (29 expect() calls)
3. `bun run tsc --noEmit` → exit 0, no errors

## Requirements Advanced

- R001 — bun run tsc --noEmit exits 0 across the full codebase including new runner and handler files

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

None. Both tasks delivered exactly as planned. The only notable implementation choice (empty addonIds early-return before enqueue) was a sensible guard not explicitly specified but aligned with the handler's existing empty-file-list behavior.

## Known Limitations

Fork PRs are not handled — the handler always clones the PR's head ref directly, which works for same-repo PRs (the common case for xbmc repos). Fork handling (clone base then fetchAndCheckoutPullRequestHeadRef) is deferred to S03 polish per the T02 plan note.

## Follow-ups

S03 should add fork PR workspace handling (clone base + fetchAndCheckoutPullRequestHeadRef) following the review.ts pattern at lines 1178-1205. The __runSubprocessForTests injection point in runAddonChecker is ready for integration testing if a fixture bad-addon directory is added later.

## Files Created/Modified

- `src/lib/addon-checker-runner.ts` — New: pure injectable subprocess runner — ANSI-stripping output parser, Kodi branch resolver, timeout-gated subprocess spawner
- `src/lib/addon-checker-runner.test.ts` — New: 19 tests covering parseCheckerOutput, resolveCheckerBranch, and runAddonChecker edge cases
- `src/handlers/addon-check.ts` — Updated: wired workspaceManager, jobQueue, runAddonChecker, resolveCheckerBranch into handler flow; workspace lifecycle with finally cleanup; per-finding and summary structured logs
- `src/handlers/addon-check.test.ts` — Updated: 6 new tests for branch resolution, workspace lifecycle, runner dispatch, finding logs, cleanup-on-error; 5 scaffold tests retained
- `src/index.ts` — Updated: passes workspaceManager and jobQueue to createAddonCheckHandler

# S03: PR comment posting and idempotency

**Wired addon findings into an idempotent PR comment upsert, added fork detection, and updated the Dockerfile — 26 tests pass, tsc clean.**

## What Happened

S03 completed in two tasks with no blockers.

T01 built `src/lib/addon-check-formatter.ts` — a pure, stateless module with two exports. `buildAddonCheckMarker(owner, repo, prNumber)` returns a deterministic HTML comment string used as the idempotency key when scanning existing PR comments. `formatAddonCheckComment(findings, marker)` renders the full comment body: marker on line 1, a `## Kodiai Addon Check` heading, a markdown table of ERROR+WARN findings (INFO filtered), and a `_X error(s), Y warning(s) found._` summary line. When there are no ERROR/WARN findings the clean-pass branch emits no table at all. `AddonFinding` is imported from the re-export in `addon-check.ts` to avoid circular deps. 11 unit tests cover all branches including empty findings, all-INFO input, multi-addon rows, zero counts, and marker placement.

T02 wired the formatter into the handler and extended the test suite. Three changes were made to `src/handlers/addon-check.ts`:

1. **Fork detection** — reads `payload.pull_request.head.repo`; sets `isFork = Boolean(headRepo && headRepo.full_name !== repo)` and `isDeletedFork = !headRepo`. Fork and deleted-fork PRs clone the base branch then call `fetchAndCheckoutPullRequestHeadRef` to overlay the PR head. Non-fork PRs continue to clone the head ref directly.

2. **Idempotent upsert** — inline `upsertAddonCheckComment` helper (unexported) lists existing PR comments, finds one whose body contains the marker, then calls `updateComment` if found or `createComment` if not. The upsert is skipped entirely when all addons returned `toolNotFound: true` (checker binary absent from the runner environment).

3. **Dockerfile** — adds `python3 python3-pip` via apt and `kodi-addon-checker` via pip3, so the checker is available in the production container.

4 new tests were added to `addon-check.test.ts` (bringing total to 15): posts-comment when findings exist, skips comment when all toolNotFound, updates existing comment on re-push (upsert path), and fork PR path. A `createMockOctokitWithIssues` helper adds `listComments`/`createComment`/`updateComment` stubs to the existing mock. The fork test uses `__fetchAndCheckoutForTests` injection mirroring the `__runSubprocessForTests` pattern established in S02.

Key non-obvious finding: `toolNotFound` is detected via caught ENOENT error (`.code === "ENOENT"`), not via exitCode:127. A subprocess exiting with 127 takes the success branch. Tests must stub the subprocess to throw with `{ code: "ENOENT" }` to exercise the skip gate.

## Verification

T01: `bun test src/lib/addon-check-formatter.test.ts` — 11 pass, 0 fail, 8ms.

# S03 UAT: PR Comment Posting and Idempotency

## Preconditions
